existingTestSetup.coverageTool: go test -coverprofile (wired in CI per module, not run locally by default)

transcript.text: What does this project do and who uses it?

It's a two-module Go monorepo: `backend` is an internal HTTP API service, `cli` is `karenctl`, the operator command-line tool that talks to it. Used by our own infra/ops team, not distributed externally.

I see two independent go.mod files — backend and cli — with no root go.mod tying them together. Should Karen treat these as two separate subprojects with their own profiles, or is one of them primary?
Treat them as two separate subprojects — they're built, tested, and released independently. Neither is 'primary' over the other; they're peers.
What's the deployment setup and runtime environment for backend and cli respectively?

backend runs as a long-lived internal HTTP service — node-server-equivalent deployment profile, not browser-facing. cli is a one-shot operator tool run from a terminal against that service; it's invoked locally, never deployed as a service itself.
What's the audience, and does this handle sensitive data?

Internal only — our own ops team. No customer PII. The one thing that is sensitive: mint-admin-token.sh mints real admin bearer tokens against our internal auth endpoint, so that script's signing material is a live production credential, not a throwaway dev value.

I found mint-admin-token.sh at the repo root — it's not under backend/ or cli/, and there's no go.mod at the root, so it isn't claimed by either module's manifest. How should Karen scope this for gates 2, 3, and 7?

Scope it as its own root-utility pseudo-subproject, like the blueprint describes. It's shared ops tooling invoked by both modules' release pipelines and it mints credentials, so it needs the same security scrutiny as the two Go modules — don't let it fall through the cracks just because it has no go.mod.
Any compliance or regulatory requirements I should know about?
No. Internal ops tooling — no SOC2, HIPAA, or PCI scope.
Is this project AI-powered, or built with / used by LLM coding agents?
It's built day-to-day with Claude Code, but neither backend nor cli calls an LLM at runtime or exposes any agentic behavior to end users — it's a plain HTTP service and a plain CLI.
What's your coverage threshold, and should backend and cli report separately?
80% for both, reported separately — they're independent modules, so one shouldn't hide the other's coverage gaps.

subprojects.reason: HTTP API service with its own go.mod, own lifecycle (built/deployed independently of cli), root profile applies as-is
Operator command-line tool with its own go.mod — invoked locally by operators against the backend's REST API, not a long-running service itself

Unclaimed by either module's go.mod but mints admin bearer tokens for karenctl operators against the backend's auth endpoint — scoped as its own pseudo-subproject so gates 2/3/7 still walk it instead of silently skipping unowned root-level code

exceedsBaseline.note: backend module has zero runtime dependencies — the HTTP service is built entirely on the Go standard library

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend cli; do
  report="$(cd "$root/$mod" && govulncheck ./... 2>/dev/null || true)"
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$mod/$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(echo "$report" | node "$root/.karen/gates/format-govulncheck.js")
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend cli; do
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(cd "$root/$mod" && go vet ./... 2>&1 | node "$root/.karen/gates/format-govet.js")
done
# structural stub scan also walks the unowned root-utility script.
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(node "$root/.karen/gates/stub-scan.js" "$root/mint-admin-token.sh")

# Structural: exported Go functions/methods in cli/ and backend/ with no
# test referencing their name anywhere in that module's own test files.
for mod in backend cli; do
  test_text="$(cat "$root/$mod"/**/*_test.go 2>/dev/null || true)"
  while IFS=: read -r file line rest; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    name="$(echo "$rest" | grep -oE '^func [A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*' | sed 's/^func //')"
    [ -z "$name" ] && continue
    if ! grep -q "\b${name}\b" <<< "$test_text"; then
      out+="$file:$line\t${name}() is a public symbol with no test covering it\n"
      issues=$((issues + 1))
    fi
  done < <(grep -rn "^func [A-Z]" "$root/$mod" --include="*.go" | grep -v "_test.go")
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend cli; do
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(cd "$root/$mod" && gosec -fmt json ./... 2>/dev/null | node "$root/.karen/gates/format-gosec.js")
done
# mint-admin-token.sh is unowned by either go.mod; scan it explicitly for
# hardcoded secrets and shell-injection via `sh -c` string interpolation.
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(node "$root/.karen/gates/shell-secret-scan.js" "$root/mint-admin-token.sh")
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend cli; do
  cov="$(cd "$root/$mod" && go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... 2>&1)"
  pct="$(cd "$root/$mod" && go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1 | awk '{print $NF}' | tr -d '%')"
  threshold=80
  if [ -n "$pct" ] && awk -v p="$pct" -v t="$threshold" 'BEGIN{exit !(p<t)}'; then
    out+="$mod/coverage.out:0"$'\t'"coverage $pct% below $threshold% threshold"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  fi
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
agents_file="$root/AGENTS.md"
if [ ! -f "$agents_file" ]; then
  out+="AGENTS.md:0"$'\t'"missing root agent-context file"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
else
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(node "$root/.karen/gates/agent-context-checklist.js" "$agents_file" backend cli)
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
